text: Gross Profit Optimization for Maximum Performance The primary goal for any business is to increase gross profit, thereby maximizing net income. The gross profit margin can significantly influence the net income. If the gross profit margin is low, then the net income will also be low, which can severely hinder the firm’s business operations and investment in expansion projects. In addition, gross profit is an indicator that can show a business’s performance and also influence the cash flow in the future. This study analyses the effect of production cost on the gross profit in each business sector. This study uses a qualitative descriptive approach by combining a general literature review of articles indexed in Sinta databases from 2018 to 2024. The analysis shows that the production cost has a significant influence on a company’s profit. It is concluded that there is a relationship between reducing the production cost, which as a result, leads to a lower cost of goods sold and hence to a high gross profit margin.
